title.value: Organocatalytic Enantioselective Construction of Axially Chiral Tetrasubstituted Allenes via 1,6-Addition of Alkynyl Indole Imine Methides with 2-Substituted Indoles

abstract.value: An organocatalytic construction of axially chiral tetrasubstituted allenes featuring indole skeletons has been established. With the aid of chiral phosphoric acid, alkynyl indole imine methides were formed in situ from α-(3-indolyl) propargylic alcohols, followed by the asymmetric 1,6-conjugate addition with 2-substituted indoles, furnishing axially chiral indole-containing tetrasubstituted allenes in generally high yields and enantioselectivities.

warnings: Product labeling is somewhat ambiguous because the paper explicitly targets axially chiral tetrasubstituted allenes; the product was classified as the stereochemically defined family 'axially chiral allenes'.

The exact transformation is an asymmetric 1,6-conjugate addition to alkynyl indole imine methides generated from propargylic alcohols; because no exact reaction-type label is available, it was mapped to 'propargylic substitution and cross-coupling'.
No allowed leaving-group label directly matches the in situ activation of propargylic alcohols, so leaving_group was set to 'not specified'.

value.ligand_or_chiral_source: chiral Brønsted acid and base catalysts
